VC

Venture Math Lab

Monte Carlo for startup financing risk

SimulatorCompareMethodology

Scenario builder

Monte Carlo Simulator

A clean seed scenario with a post-money SAFE, target pool refreshes, and moderate pro rata behavior.

Guidance for every input

Each control now includes plain-English guidance so you can change assumptions on phone, tablet, or desktop without needing the methodology page open beside it.

Scenario Setup

Start from a real benchmark, then tune only the assumptions that matter for this company.

Seed benchmark

Median pre-money $15,800,000, median round $3,800,000, target option pool 14%.

SAFE and note overhang begin to matter because the Series A sets the first clean ownership picture.

Current valuation and ownership

These inputs define the cap table before future rounds start compounding dilution.

Instrument terms

Keep this standard-friendly. The simulator explains complex term-sheet tricks but does not fully model them.

Stakeholder controls

These inputs determine how much the current investor can defend ownership and how much value employees must create to clear exercise cost.

Ownership input total: 100.0%. The simulator normalizes the mix if it is not exactly 100%.

Run a scenario to see founder, employee, and investor distributions.

Results surface

Founder, employee, and investor views

Last run ready

Run the simulation to see how dilution, down rounds, SAFE or note conversion, preferences, and partial secondary liquidity change outcomes across stakeholders.
